columns.extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Darolutamide. This is explicitly stated in the abstract on page 1: "Patients with metastatic hormone-sensitive prostate cancer were randomly assigned to darolutamide or placebo plus androgen-deprivation therapy and docetaxel." This indicates that darolutamide (or placebo) is added to the standard backbone of ADT and docetaxel. The "Trial Design" section on page 2 further confirms this: "Enrolled patients were randomly assigned (1:1) to oral darolutamide 600 mg twice daily or matched placebo, in combination with ADT and docetaxel."
